title: Why Civilization I, II, III Matters
body:
It matters because the biggest forces in the world often come from the infrastructure of civilisation rather than from single events. Energy, logistics, data, and governance all shape what a society can do.

The lens also matters because it makes the future feel legible. If you can see what kind of civilisation is emerging, you can think more clearly about risk, opportunity, and long-term direction.
keyPoints: It links history to future planning
It shows why infrastructure changes politics
It helps compare old and new power structures
furtherReading: Vaclav Smil, 'How the World Really Works'
